propositions: Parallel agent sessions race to land on fast-forward-only main; the loser must manually rebase-to-tip and retry, causing redundant CI re-runs on non-conflicting changes.

This epic builds a serial auto-lander so agents set an Autosubmit opt-in label and walk away while the front submittable change/chain rebases to tip, awaits fresh Verified, and ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA.

The agent contract is one command returning one typed terminal outcome (merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed | review_failed) with the bot as single source of truth, so agents never correlate Gerrit votes/labels/status themselves.
The two-vote gate is never weakened: nothing lands whose to-be-merged tree wasn't CI-tested, FFO and strict Verified copyCondition are preserved, and GerriScary/CVE-2025-1568 posture is audited.
Landing is platform-specific and maintainer-owned, kept in infra/ plus project tooling with no Gerrit-specific landing logic added to src/rebar core (may import rebar only for ticket ops).
Cross-stack batching (Phase 2) was evaluated and rejected; throughput is deferred to bounded speculation as a separate idea, promoted only on a measured serial-treadmill pain.
